gettingStarted.title: Elysium
gettingStarted.subtitle: Getting Started Guide
gettingStarted.description: Elysium requires a local HTTP server to function properly due to browser security policies. Please select your platform below to view the setup instructions.
gettingStarted.whyText: Modern browsers block certain features (like modules and fetch API) when opening files directly (file://). Running a local server solves this.
gettingStarted.winStep2: Open Command Prompt (cmd) in the folder containing index.html.
gettingStarted.winOpt2Step1: Install "HTTP Server" from Microsoft Store (e.g., by phlox).
gettingStarted.winOpt2Step2: Open the app and select the Elysium folder.
gettingStarted.macText: Python is usually pre-installed. Open Terminal in the project folder and run:
gettingStarted.macHint: If Python is missing, install it via Homebrew (brew install python) or your package manager (sudo apt install python3).
gettingStarted.androidInst3: Choose the folder where index.html is located.
gettingStarted.androidInst4: Set "http://localhost:8000" in the settings. Click "Start server".
gettingStarted.androidInst5: Open the browser and go to the address
gettingStarted.iosInst2: Copy the Elysium project files into the Documents app.
gettingStarted.iosInst3: Open Documents, go to Settings (Gear icon).
gettingStarted.iosInst5: The app will display a URL. Open Safari and navigate to that URL.
gettingStarted.footer: After starting the server, open http://localhost:8000 (or the address shown by the app) in your browser.

userAgreement.title: User Agreement
userAgreement.section1: I. Elysium provides an opportunity for online communication among users.
userAgreement.section2: II. Elysium does not collect, transfer, or use your personal data to third parties.
userAgreement.section3: III. Elysium does not store your personal data on third-party servers.
userAgreement.section4Title: IV. To store conversations, one of the available server options is used. You are entitled to choose any available under your jurisdiction:
userAgreement.section5: V. Elysium uses encryption methods that prevent the content of your conversation from being read without a special key, which must be held by each participant.
userAgreement.section6: VI. Elysium is not responsible for your data in case of loss of the access key or deletion of the conversation.
userAgreement.section7Title: VII. Elysium allows you to send:
userAgreement.section9: VIII. You can securely save the entire conversation to your device in JSON format. Decryption of your conversation will occur strictly on your device.
userAgreement.section10: IX. The program is distributed "AS IS", therefore the user assumes all responsibility for its use.

warningPage.title: Connection
slogans: Deleting does not mean erasing.
If rushed for an answer, it means no time to think is given. Stop.
A personal secret is someone else's power.
Adults seeking friends among children online always hide their faces.
A screenshot is taken instantly.
A sent photo remains in someone else's hands forever. Is it worth the risk?
Doubts? End the conversation.
The words "tell no one" are the first step into a trap.
The illusion of safety kills.
No one gives money for nothing on the internet. Everything has a price.
Intrusive care is just bait.
Manipulators always say what one wants to hear, not the truth.
Own naivety is someone else's profit.
If communication causes anxiety, it's not intuition playing tricks, it's a real threat.
"Just between us" is a trap.
Blackmail starts with one little secret that is scary to reveal.
Who is the communication really with?
Anyone can hide behind a fake avatar. Trust no one.
Take a moment to think. At least a minute.
Real friends do not demand proof of trust in the form of personal photos.
